description_long: This object is a pointed, leaf-shaped stone with a roughly symmetrical outline and a sharp tip at one end. Its surface is mottled with shades of gray, white, and subtle brownish hues, giving it a natural, weathered appearance. The stone is relatively thin and tapers to a fine edge along its sides, suggesting it may have been shaped or chipped for a specific purpose. One side features handwritten markings in black ink, possibly for cataloging or identification. The texture appears rough and uneven, with visible chips and irregularities typical of flint or chert. Overall, the stone has a primitive, utilitarian look, reminiscent of an ancient tool or artifact.
description: A pointed, leaf-shaped stone with a mottled gray and white surface, rough texture, and visible chipping. One side bears handwritten markings, adding to its artifact-like appearance.
description_view_0: The broad, flat face of the stone is visible, showing its pointed tip, rough surface, and mottled coloration.
description_view_1: A narrow edge-on view reveals the thinness and sharpness of the stone, highlighting its blade-like profile.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is shown, featuring handwritten black markings and similar surface texture to the front.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the stone's thin, elongated form.
attribute_location: archaeological site, urban park, or landscaped garden
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in soil or gravel
attribute_pattern: Mottled pattern with irregular gray and white patches, some subtle brown veining, and visible chipping; no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and chipped with minor surface irregularities, indicating moderate weathering but not extensive erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smooth, man-made surfaces but can blend with natural gravel or stone features; may be partially covered by soil or leaf litter in outdoor settings.
